channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is reshaping transgender people’s rights
item.description: Coming up at 9 a.m. on Tuesday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests break down President Donald Trump’s series of executive orders that significantly reshape transgender rights in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Nikhil Kumaran, MPR News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE access of recipient information: AP sources
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting commissioner has stepped down from her role at the agency over Department of Government Efficiency requests to access Social Security recipient information.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ex-Watergate prosecutor urges judge to reject request to drop charges against NYC mayor
item.description: A former Watergate prosecutor has urged the federal judge presiding over the prosecution of New York City Mayor Eric Adams to assign a special counsel to help decide how to handle the Justice Department's request to drop charges.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boston police commissioner doubles down on ICE resistance: 'We don’t enforce' detainers
item.description: Democrat-appointed Boston Police Commissioner Michael Cox is echoing Mayor Michelle Wu's pledged resistance to the Trump administration's deportations, saying on a local news station on Sunday that "we don’t enforce" civil immigration detainers filed by ICE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP lawmakers divided on Musk, seen by some as a liability
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are divided over tech billionaire Elon Musk being the public face of President Trump’s aggressive bid to shrink the government, with some souring on Musk’s prominent role as he increasingly has become a target of Democratic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: The anti-Musk protest movement is expected to ramp up with Congress on recess
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump is the president, but billionaire Elon Musk is the focus for thousands of Democratic activists launching a protest campaign this week to fight the Trump administration's push to gut federal health, education and human services agencies.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to let the firing of whistleblower agency head proceed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to permit the firing of the head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, according to documents obtained Sunday that would mark the first appeal to the justices since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Proposed bill looks to make President Trump’s birthday a federal holiday
item.description: Tenney’s press release noted that this proposed bill would “permanently codify a new federal holiday.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, NewsNation

item.title: NYC funding for migrant hotels should go to Helene victims: NC sheriff
item.description: FEMA has taken more than $80 million in total from New York City, escalating a dispute between Trump and NYC.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event. It's part of an ongoing terminology dispute over the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How changes to a CDC vaccine panel under Kennedy could reshape policy
item.description: A committee of experts that advises the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is critical in setting national vaccine policy. It's also vulnerable to political interference.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
